Headline: Pepperstone expands Perpetual CFDs to Gold, Silver, Nasdaq, S&P 500, WTI, and Brent Crude with 24/7 trading — globally regulated CFD broker with 700,000+ active clients predominantly in APAC and MENA explicitly modelling perpetual CFDs on crypto's 24/7 ethos with no identified USDT deposit infrastructure
Why It Matters: Pepperstone is a globally regulated CFD broker (ASIC, FCA, CySEC, DFSA, CMA) serving 700,000+ active clients concentrated in APAC and MENA — the two highest-USDT-demand regions globally. Its perpetual CFD expansion to commodities and indices explicitly models the product on crypto's 24/7 trading ethos, signalling a product team actively bridging traditional finance and crypto-native client expectations. No institutional-grade B2B USDT/USDC multichain settlement or EUR SEPA/SWIFT fiat off-ramp has been identified in Pepperstone's payment infrastructure. GWC's 300+ asset multichain rails directly address the deposit gap for Pepperstone's APAC and MENA clients who are USDT-native and currently lack a native crypto deposit rail for their accounts.
Suggested Action: Research decision-maker at Pepperstone — target Head of Payments or VP Product for USDT multichain deposit rail pitch during active product innovation cycle; reference Perpetual CFD 24/7 launch and APAC/MENA client concentration as the entry point for a crypto payment infrastructure discussion

Headline: OCC grants final approval for Circle's First National Digital Currency Bank N.A. / Circle National Trust on July 10, 2026 — USDC now has federally-regulated US custody infrastructure, amplifying USDC's MiCA-compliant credibility for EU Forex brokers post-July 1
Why It Matters: The OCC's final approval for Circle National Trust gives USDC federally-regulated US custody infrastructure for the first time — the single largest credibility signal for institutional USDC adoption since MiCA. For EU Forex brokers navigating MiCA's post-July 1 stablecoin requirements, USDC is now the only MiCA-compliant e-money stablecoin with a federally-chartered US trust company as its custodian. GWC's EUR SEPA/SWIFT fiat off-ramp and USDC multichain settlement rails position GWC as the natural B2B infrastructure layer for EU and UK Forex brokers accelerating USDC adoption under MiCA — Circle's federal charter removes the last institutional objection to USDC as a primary stablecoin settlement currency.
Suggested Action: Use Circle OCC approval as a conversation-opener in outreach to EU and UK Forex brokers evaluating MiCA-compliant stablecoin payment infrastructure; frame GWC's USDC multichain rails as the ready B2B settlement layer now that USDC has federally-regulated US custody

Headline: Interactive Brokers adds bidirectional stablecoin funding (USDC, PYUSD, RLUSD) on July 14, 2026 — largest US multi-asset broker enabling stablecoin deposits and withdrawals across its global brokerage platform, excluding UK and Irish clients
Why It Matters: Interactive Brokers enabling bidirectional stablecoin funding (USDC, PYUSD, RLUSD) for its global client base marks the clearest signal yet that stablecoin deposits are becoming a baseline expectation at tier-1 multi-asset brokers. Mid-tier Forex and CFD brokers watching IB's move face a client defection risk if they lack comparable USDT/USDC deposit rails — their clients who want stablecoin funding will migrate to platforms that offer it. GWC's 300+ asset USDT/USDC multichain deposit processing and EUR SEPA/SWIFT fiat off-ramp are the B2B payment infrastructure these mid-tier brokers need to match IB's stablecoin capability without building in-house.
Suggested Action: Use Interactive Brokers' stablecoin funding launch as a market signal in outreach to mid-tier Forex and CFD brokers without USDT deposit rails — frame GWC's multichain API as the ready answer to IB's new competitive pressure before client defection occurs

Headline: Revolut receives UAE VARA in-principle approval for crypto broker-dealer and exchange services on July 15, 2026 — 75M-customer neobank accelerating USDT/crypto access for UAE retail clients alongside Forex broker competitors
Why It Matters: Revolut's VARA in-principle approval for crypto broker-dealer and exchange services in the UAE accelerates USDT/crypto adoption among UAE retail clients of Forex brokers — the MENA market where USDT is already the dominant deposit method. As Revolut's 75M global customers increasingly access USDT via Revolut's UAE crypto services, UAE Forex brokers without a USDT deposit rail face a growing gap between what clients use for everyday stablecoin transactions and what brokers accept for account funding. GWC's USDT multichain deposit rails and EUR SEPA/SWIFT fiat off-ramp address this client acquisition gap for UAE Forex brokers before Revolut's full licence activates.
Suggested Action: Note market context — use Revolut VARA approval as a talking point in outreach to UAE Forex brokers lacking USDT deposit infrastructure; reference growing USDT accessibility in the UAE as client demand evidence for GWC's multichain payment rails

Headline: Merkur Group acquires US-regulated iGaming content supplier White Hat Studios (active in NJ, PA, MI, WV, CT, DE, RI) on July 15, 2026 — European gaming conglomerate building US-regulated digital content footprint with no crypto payment processor identified
Why It Matters: Merkur Group (German gaming conglomerate, owner of Betcenter Belgium) acquires White Hat Studios, an iGaming content supplier regulated across seven US states — giving Merkur a direct entry into the licensed US iGaming market as a content provider. US-regulated iGaming content suppliers trigger B2B settlement flows between operators and studios with no dominant crypto payment processor identified in either entity. GWC's USDT/USDC multichain settlement and EUR SEPA/SWIFT fiat off-ramp address the cross-border B2B payment layer between White Hat Studios' US operator partners and Merkur Group's European treasury — the acquisition creates a new cross-Atlantic settlement use case.
Suggested Action: Research decision-maker at White Hat Studios or Merkur Group digital division — target Head of Finance or VP of Operations for USDT/USDC B2B settlement pitch for cross-border operator payment flows between US regulated markets and European treasury; reference acquisition as the cross-Atlantic settlement trigger

Headline: EDGE Markets raises $29.2M Series A (CoinFund-led) to build EDGE Connect (retail prediction market deposit rail) and EDGE Pro (institutional deposit accounts for market makers) — direct competition with crypto payment rails for prediction market operators
Why It Matters: EDGE Markets is a $29.2M-funded competitor building dedicated payment infrastructure specifically for prediction markets — two competing products (EDGE Connect for retail operators and EDGE Pro for institutional market makers) that target GWC's B2B payment rail positioning in the prediction market segment. GWC differentiators: 300+ asset USDT/USDC multichain breadth vs EDGE's prediction-market-only focus; EUR SEPA/SWIFT fiat off-ramp for European operator settlement (EDGE lacks confirmed EU coverage); live track record across iGaming and Forex broker verticals vs EDGE's niche focus. For prediction market platform clients approaching payment selection, GWC's multi-vertical API avoids the vertical lock-in that EDGE's specialisation creates.
Suggested Action: Note competitive context — update GWC pitch to highlight 300+ asset multichain breadth, live multi-vertical iGaming/Forex track record, and EUR SEPA/SWIFT fiat off-ramp vs EDGE Markets' prediction-market-only payment rails; prepare direct comparison card for prediction market operator prospects approaching payment selection
